name: ralph-tui-prd
description: Generate a Product Requirements Document (PRD) for ralph-tui task orchestration. Creates PRDs with user stories that can be converted to beads issues or prd.json for automated execution. Triggers on: create a prd, write prd for, plan this feature, requirements for, spec out.
qualityReason: Actionable PRD creation for task orchestration
capabilities: Guides users through clarifying questions to capture feature requirements
Generates structured PRDs with user stories, goals, and acceptance criteria
Defines quality gates that must pass for each story
Creates unambiguous functional requirements and out-of-scope items
Formats output for ralph-tui task orchestration tool
author: subsy
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The file is pure markdown documentation (SKILL.md) for generating Product Requirements Documents. The detected backticks are markdown formatting for displaying example commands, not shell execution. No cryptographic operations or dangerous code patterns exist in this documentation file.
valueStatement: This skill helps product teams create detailed Product Requirements Documents for AI agent execution. It guides users through clarifying questions about feature goals, scope, and quality gates, then generates structured PRDs with user stories and acceptance criteria.
limitations: Does not implement features - only creates planning documents
Does not access external systems or APIs
Does not execute code or run commands
Requires user input to answer clarifying questions

useCases.title: New Feature Planning
useCases.description: Product managers use this skill to transform feature ideas into detailed PRDs that AI agents can execute, ensuring nothing is missed during implementation.

useCases.title: Complex Feature Scoping
useCases.description: Development teams use the skill to break down complex features into small, independently completable user stories with clear acceptance criteria.

useCases.title: Cross-Team Alignment
useCases.description: Teams create shared documentation that serves as a single source of truth for stakeholders, developers, and AI agents executing the work.

promptTemplates.title: Basic PRD Request
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new feature that needs structured requirements documentation

promptTemplates.title: Refined Feature Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a PRD for adding [feature] to [product/context]. The main goal is to [business objective].
promptTemplates.scenario: When you have some context about the feature but need structured exploration

promptTemplates.title: Detailed PRD Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a PRD for [feature]. Target users are [user type]. Key requirements: [brief list]. Quality commands: [specific commands that must pass].
promptTemplates.scenario: When you have partial requirements and want to fill gaps through Q&A

promptTemplates.title: Quick PRD from Issue
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a PRD based on this issue description: [paste issue content]. Ask me clarifying questions if needed.
promptTemplates.scenario: Converting a GitHub issue or bug report into a full PRD

bestPractices: Answer all clarifying questions thoroughly - each answer helps create a more accurate PRD
Specify quality gates early so stories include proper verification steps
Keep user stories small enough to complete in one focused session
antiPatterns: Providing minimal information and expecting a complete PRD without Q&A
Requesting implementation alongside PRD creation - this skill only plans
Skipping quality gates - they are critical for story verification

faq.question: What triggers this skill?
faq.answer: Use phrases like 'create a prd', 'write prd for', 'plan this feature', 'requirements for', or 'spec out'.

faq.question: Does this skill write code?
faq.answer: No, this skill only generates Product Requirements Documents. Other skills or the ralph-tui tool handle implementation.

faq.question: How many questions will I be asked?
faq.answer: Typically 2-4 rounds of questions. Each round covers a different aspect like goals, scope, users, and quality gates.

faq.question: What are quality gates?
faq.answer: Commands that must pass for every user story, such as type checking, linting, or tests. They ensure code quality across the implementation.

faq.question: How small should user stories be?
faq.answer: Each story should be completable in one focused AI agent session, typically 2-4 hours of work. Large features become multiple stories.

faq.question: What happens after the PRD is generated?
faq.answer: The PRD is wrapped in [PRD] markers for ralph-tui. Conversion tools can transform it into beads issues or a prd.json file for execution.
